hero.title: The Art of Painting Miniatures
hero.subtitle: Explore a world where every brushstroke tells a story, every shadow comes to life, and each miniature is a tiny universe ready to shine.

welcome.title: Welcome to the Fascinating World of Miniatures
welcome.text1: Painting miniatures is not just a hobby; it's miniature art. Every brushstroke brings life, every shading or gradient transforms the piece from a static object into a character with a story. Here you'll learn how each technique can tell its own narrative, from an epic warrior to a tiny villain with details that will make you smile.
welcome.tip: Tip: Patience is your best ally, and yes… there will be hours when you think, 'Why did I get into this?' But when you finish a miniature, the satisfaction is indescribable 😏.

techniques.title: Exploring Painting Techniques
techniques.text1: From dry brushing to washes, glazes, and gradients, each technique adds a different atmosphere to the miniature. A well-applied wash can highlight armor textures, while a smooth gradient can bring robes and capes to life. It's not just painting: it's how color, light, and shadow tell the story of the character you're creating.
techniques.tip: Advice: Always keep your paint well-diluted and experiment with light layers; less is more when aiming for realism. And don’t worry if you make a mess on the table—it's part of the process! 🎨

gallery.title: Completed Miniatures Gallery
gallery.subtitle: See how tiny details come to life in these finished miniatures. Each piece is a mix of technique, patience, and creativity. Even details that seem insignificant, like a scar or a shine on the armor, can make a figure stand out.

fun.title: Miniatures with Personality and Humor
fun.text1: Some miniatures have their own character. Perhaps a hero with an exaggerated expression, a monster that seems about to crack a joke, or a comical scene that makes you smile even before finishing the piece. Adding humor or small narratives in the details makes your collection unique.
fun.tip: Note: Keep your cat away from the table while painting; it might steal brushes or leave unexpected 'artistic paw prints' 😹.

hero.title: About Me
hero.subtitle: Painting miniatures is for me much more than a hobby. It's creativity, patience, constant learning… and above all, fun.

history.title: How It All Started
history.p1: Like many miniature enthusiasts, it all started with a simple figure, some basic brushes, and a few paints. At first, I didn’t really know what I was doing; I was just trying not to ruin the miniature too much.
history.p2: But little by little, I discovered something fascinating: in a figure just a few centimeters tall, you can create a complete scene. You can tell a story with colors, shadows, and tiny details that almost go unnoticed at first glance.
history.p3: That's when I realized that painting miniatures is a perfect mix of hobby, craftsmanship, and art.
history.p4: And yes… it also means spending ten minutes staring at a shoulder pad, wondering if that highlight should be just a little brighter.

process.title: The Creative Process
process.p1: What I enjoy most about painting miniatures is the process. From the moment you choose the miniature to the final tiny details, each step has something special.
process.p2: Thinking about the color scheme, trying new techniques, experimenting with shadows and lights… each miniature becomes a small artistic project.
process.p3: Sometimes it comes out perfect on the first try. Other times, you have to redo, correct, or even start over. But that is exactly what makes the hobby so interesting.
process.p4: There is always something new to learn.

philosophy.title: What I Love Most About the Hobby
philosophy.p1: Painting miniatures combines many things I love: creativity, focus, and a certain obsession with details. It’s an activity that forces you to slow down, notice small things, and enjoy the process.
philosophy.p2: In a world where everything moves fast, painting miniatures is almost the opposite. You sit down, put on music or a podcast, grab your brush… and for a few hours, only the miniature in front of you exists.
philosophy.p3: It’s the perfect mix of relaxation and creative challenge.

community.title: The Miniature Painting Community
community.p1: One of the things I value most about the miniature world is the community. There are thousands of painters sharing techniques, tutorials, tips, and projects. Thanks to that, we all improve.
community.p2: I have learned so much from other painters who generously shared their knowledge. Videos, guides, advice on forums or social media… all of that is part of how this hobby evolves.
community.p3: That’s why I try to give back a little of what I’ve received.
community.p4: If someone is just starting out and needs help, I’m always happy to share what I know. We all start with fairly… questionable miniatures, but with practice and good advice, progress comes sooner than you think.

teaching.title: Helping Other Painters
teaching.p1: I enjoy teaching techniques, explaining processes, and helping other painters improve. There’s nothing more satisfying than seeing someone progress in the hobby thanks to a small tip or an explanation that helps them understand a technique better.
teaching.p2: Sometimes it’s enough to learn how to dilute paint properly, understand how shadows work, or lose the fear of experimenting with colors.
teaching.p3: The hobby is much more fun when shared.

closure.title: The Journey Continues
closure.p1: This portfolio is simply a way to share a part of that journey. Some miniatures represent techniques I’ve already mastered, others are experiments and projects where I’m trying new things.
closure.p2: The important thing is not painting the perfect miniature, but continuing to learn, improve, and enjoy the creative process.
closure.p3: Because in the end, every miniature tells a story… and every painter has their own.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: If you have any questions or want to discuss a project, feel free to send me a message.
contact.intro1: I have been painting miniatures for more than 10 years, constantly learning new techniques and exploring different styles. What started as a hobby slowly became a passion for detail, creativity and storytelling through tiny figures.
contact.intro2: If you have any questions about painting miniatures, techniques, materials, or even where to start, feel free to ask. No question is too small or too simple — everyone starts somewhere and I'm always happy to help.
contact.intro3: Whether you're just getting into the hobby or you've been painting for years, I'm always glad to talk about miniatures, share advice, and connect with other hobbyists.
